title: NOC 93100 – Central control and process operators, mineral and metal processing | GTR Immigration
description: In 2026, Canada’s primary metal and mineral sectors are central to the global "Critical Minerals" strategy. Central Control Operators (NOC 93100) are the technical nerve centers of refineries and steel mills, managing high-stakes, automated environments that produce the raw materials for EVs and sustainable infrastructure.

classification.teer: FeatureDetailsBroad Occupational Category9 – Occupations in manufacturing and utilitiesTEER Category2 (Occupations usually requiring a college diploma or apprenticeship)Major Group93 – Central control and process operatorsSub-Major Group931 – Central control and process operators in processing and manufacturing

common_job_titles: Central Control Room Operator – Primary Metal Processing
duties: Operating and monitoring multi-function central process control machinery to grind, separate, filter, melt, or refine mineral ores.
Observing computer printouts, video monitors, and gauges to verify processing conditions and make necessary adjustments.
Coordinating and supervising production crews, including machine and process operators.
Maintaining production logs and reporting equipment malfunctions.
employment_requirements: Education: Secondary school completion is required; a College Diploma in mineral processing or chemical technology is increasingly mandatory for new hires in 2026.
Experience: 3–5 years of experience as a machine or process operator in the same industry is typically required.
Certification: Industry-specific safety certifications (e.g., WHMIS, Internal Quality Audit) are standard.
job_outlook: The market remains balanced with a slight shortage trend as the "Critical Minerals" push intensifies. Retirements are expected to open over 2,000 positions nationally by 2030.
labour_market_demand: Ontario: Moderate to high demand driven by the "Battery Belt" expansion and primary metal manufacturing in regions like Hamilton and Sudbury.
Quebec: Strong prospects in the Abitibi-Témiscamingue and Côte-Nord regions due to increased aluminum and iron ore processing.
British Columbia: Growing demand for copper and zinc refining operators to support clean-tech exports.
Why Work in Canada as a Central Control Operator?
Economic Resilience: These roles are tied to Canada’s long-term resource strategy, offering high job security.
Top-Tier Compensation: Among the highest-paying industrial roles in Canada due to the technical complexity involved.
PR Priority: Eligible for Federal Skilled Worker and often featured in PNP draws for industrial hubs.
Advanced Tech: Work with state-of-the-art AI-driven monitoring and industrial IoT (IIoT) systems.
pathways: Express Entry: Qualifying under TEER 2 for FSW and CEC.
PNP: Priority in Ontario’s In-Demand Skills and BC PNP Tech/Resource streams.
